documents: Python is a versatile programming language used for AI and backend web development.
The sun is approximately 93 million miles away from Earth.
JavaScript is the most widely used language for building interactive web applications.
TypeScript adds static typing to JavaScript, helping developers catch errors at compile time.
Rust is a systems programming language focused on safety, speed, and concurrency.
Go was developed at Google and is known for its simplicity and strong concurrency support.
Docker allows developers to package applications and dependencies into portable containers.
Kubernetes is an open-source platform for automating deployment and scaling of containerized apps.
Git is a distributed version control system widely used in modern software development.
REST is an architectural style for designing networked applications over HTTP.
GraphQL is a query language for APIs that allows clients to request exactly the data they need.
Machine learning enables computers to learn patterns from data without being explicitly programmed.
Deep learning is a subset of machine learning that uses multi-layered neural networks.
Large language models like GPT are trained on massive text corpora to generate human-like text.
Vector embeddings represent text, images, or audio as numerical vectors in high-dimensional space.
Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) combines vector search with LLMs to ground responses in data.
PostgreSQL is a powerful open-source relational database known for its reliability and features.
MongoDB is a popular NoSQL document database that stores data in flexible JSON-like documents.
Redis is an in-memory data store often used as a cache, message broker, or session store.
The Eiffel Tower is located in Paris, France, and was completed in 1889.
Mount Everest is the highest mountain above sea level, standing at 8,848 meters.
The Amazon rainforest produces a significant share of the world's oxygen and biodiversity.
Water boils at 100 degrees Celsius at standard atmospheric pressure.
DNA carries the genetic instructions used in the growth and reproduction of all living organisms.
The speed of light in a vacuum is approximately 299,792 kilometers per second.
Jupiter is the largest planet in our solar system and has dozens of known moons.
Black holes are regions of spacetime where gravity is so strong that nothing can escape them.
Photosynthesis is the process by which plants convert sunlight into chemical energy.
The human brain contains roughly 86 billion neurons connected by trillions of synapses.
World War II ended in 1945 after the surrender of Germany and Japan.
Leonardo da Vinci painted the Mona Lisa in the early 16th century.
The Great Wall of China stretches thousands of kilometers across northern China.
Shakespeare wrote dozens of plays, including Hamlet, Macbeth, and Romeo and Juliet.
The Olympic Games originated in ancient Greece around 776 BC.
Basketball was invented by Dr. James Naismith in 1891 in Springfield, Massachusetts.
Soccer, also known as football, is the most popular sport in the world.
Coffee is one of the most widely traded commodities in the global economy.
Sushi is a traditional Japanese dish that typically combines vinegared rice with seafood.
Chroma DB is an open-source vector database designed specifically for LLM applications.
Regular exercise and a balanced diet are key components of a healthy lifestyle.
